name: Generate email newsletters from Telegram keywords with Dumpling AI and GPT

parameters.toolDescription: use this to Search Google News using the autocomplete suggestions as input. Use this after getting results from Google_autocomplete to find recent news articles on those suggested queries.
use this to Fetche autocomplete suggestions from Google based on a keyword and returns the response data

nodes.name: AI Agent: Expand Keyword & Orchestrate Tools

options.systemMessage:
=You are a helpful assistant connected to multiple tools in n8n. Your main job is to take a keyword provided by the trigger, expand it into relevant search terms, and then find the most recent news related to those terms. Here is how you should use the tools:

Google_autocomplete tool (Dumpling AI Autocomplete):

Always call this first when you receive a keyword from the trigger.

Input: the keyword or phrase provided.

Output: a list of popular search suggestions related to that keyword.

Purpose: to understand what people are actually searching for and generate multiple angles of interest on the keyword.

Google_News tool (Dumpling AI Google News):

After you receive autocomplete suggestions, pass those suggestions directly into the Google_News tool.

Input: each autocomplete suggestion as a search term.

Output: recent articles, headlines, or updates from Google News related to those suggestions.

Purpose: to gather fresh and relevant information tied to what people are searching for.

Flow Logic:

Trigger sends a keyword → Call Google_autocomplete → Take results → Feed results into Google_News → Return the final set of news articles.

If no autocomplete results are found, use the original keyword directly in Google_News.

Guidelines:

Do not skip the autocomplete step unless it returns no results.

Use the autocomplete suggestions exactly as they are for Google News searches.

Always structure your output in a clean and easy-to-read format so results can be reused in later steps of the automation.

Return result in a structured JSON,here is the example JSON:
{
  "articles": [
    {
      "category": "string",
      "title": "string",
      "url": "string",
      "source": "string",
      "summary": "string",
      "published": "string"
    }
  ]
}

values.content:
=You are an assistant that writes newsletters from provided source content. Use only the content I give you. Do not invent facts. If something is missing, omit it.

GOAL:
Create a high quality newsletter in HTML and a strong email subject line.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Return only valid JSON with this exact schema and nothing else:
{
  "newsletter": "html",
  "subject": "string"
}

SUBJECT RULES:
• Write a clear subject under 60 characters.
• Make it specific and benefit driven.
• No clickbait. No emojis. Use simple grammar.

NEWSLETTER HTML RULES:
• Produce production ready HTML suitable for email.
• Include a clear header title, a short intro paragraph, two to six story sections, and a closing call to action.
• Use simple inline CSS only. No external styles, no scripts.
• Use a single column layout with readable fonts, clear spacing, and mobile friendly widths.
• For each story, include a headline, two to three sentence summary, and a source link if present in the input.
• Group similar items together and remove duplicates.
• Keep links as plain anchor tags. Open in same tab.
• Add a brief footer with sources list using the available links from the input, and an unsubscribe placeholder.

TONE AND STYLE:
• Write in clear, friendly, professional language.
• Keep sentences short and direct.
• Avoid jargon and filler.

CONSTRAINTS:
• Use only information found in INPUT_CONTENT.
• If there is conflicting info, pick the most recent or most credible item and note it briefly.
• If there are no valid items, return an empty newsletter with a short note in the intro explaining that no recent updates were found.

VALIDATION:
• Ensure the JSON is valid.
• Escape quotes inside the HTML as needed.
• Do not include markdown. Do not include code fences. Output JSON only.

parameters.content:
## Agent Branch

This branch begins when a keyword is received from **Telegram**.  
The keyword is expanded into search queries using **Dumpling AI Autocomplete**, then relevant articles are fetched via **Dumpling AI Google News**.  

The articles are parsed into structured JSON and passed into the **Split Out** node, which separates them for further processing.

## Newsletter Branch

Starting from the **Loop Over Items** node, each article is scraped and cleaned using **Dumpling AI Scraper**.  
The cleaned articles are aggregated, then sent to **OpenAI** to generate a professional HTML newsletter and subject line.  

Finally, the newsletter is delivered via **Gmail** to the specified inbox.
